commands.review_summary:
The PR catches thrown and not-ok transcript mirror failures after `deliverOutboundPayloads` has already sent channel payloads, logs a warning, and adds two regression tests.

PR surface: Source +22, Tests +65. Total +87 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main can send the channel message before the transcript mirror append fails, and the sub-agent direct completion path treats the thrown send as undelivered. I did not run the live Telegram/Raspberry Pi scenario.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is contributor real behavior proof or maintainer proof override, not an automation-repairable code defect.

target.title: fix(outbound): keep channel send durable when transcript mirror fails (#89626)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #89626. The post-delivery transcript **mirror** in `deliverOutboundPayloads`
runs *after* the channel payloads are already sent. Its append was unguarded, so a
transient session-lock failure (`session file changed while embedded prompt lock was
released`) propagated out of the send. The whole send was then reported as `failed`
— discarding the fact that the channel message had already been delivered — and the
sub-agent announce layer retried, re-delivering the message up to **3 times** before
giving up.

The mirror is best-effort local bookkeeping. This change catches both thrown errors
and `{ ok: false }` results, logs a warning, and lets the already-successful channel
delivery stand. This matches the existing cron delivery path
(`mirrorDirectDeliveryIntoTranscript` in `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts`),
so it is consistent with the sibling surface rather than a one-sided patch. The append
still carries its idempotency key, so any later reconciliation stays deduplicated.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: 3x duplicate sub-agent completion announcements when the transcript mirror fails on retry
Real environment tested: local source checkout (macOS, Node 22.22.3, pnpm 11.2.2)
Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  pnpm test src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts
  pnpm test src/infra/outbound/message.test.ts src/infra/outbound/outbound-send-service.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts
  pnpm tsgo:core
  pnpm tsgo:test:src
  oxfmt --check + run-oxlint.mjs on changed files
Evidence after fix: 95/95 deliver tests pass (2 new regression tests); 126/126 sibling tests pass; tsgo core + test-src exit 0; format/lint clean. The two new tests fail without this patch (the thrown mirror error propagates and the send is reported failed) and pass with it.
Observed result after fix: a thrown or not-ok transcript mirror no longer fails the send; the channel handler is invoked exactly once.
What was not tested: live Telegram + Raspberry Pi sub-agent run (no such hardware available). Reproduced deterministically at the failure boundary via unit tests instead.

## Files
- `src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts` — guard the post-delivery transcript mirror append
- `src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts` — 2 regression tests + tighten the transcript mock type
target.author: harjothkhara
